details.body:
finding_summary: The idempotency guard reuses any bead whose normalized title matches, even though it is searching across `br list --all`, so a generic title like `Core`, `Validation`, or `Add exporter smoke test` can cause this export to adopt an unrelated or closed bead from another milestone and then wire it under the new root/workstream hierarchy.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1491-1504
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1669-1693
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:366-373
remediation_hint: Restrict reuse to beads that are already provably owned by this milestone export (for example via a persisted milestone-specific marker/label/comment plus the expected parent chain), and reject ambiguous, closed, or type-mismatched title matches instead of blindly popping the first summary.

finding_summary: The export path ends with an unconditional `sync_flush()`, but this command is created from a fresh `BrMutationAdapter` and never performs the existing ownership/health checks before flushing. Because `sync_flush()` clears all pending mutation markers, an `export-beads` run can publish someone else's unsynced local bead edits that were already present in `.beads/.br-unsynced-mutations.d/`.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1647-1650
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1423-1432
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1556-1562
remediation_hint: Use the guarded sync path (`sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy`) or fail fast before mutating when foreign pending mutation records exist, so this workflow only ever flushes its own export work.

finding_summary: Created bead IDs are resolved by taking the first token-like suffix from `br create` stdout, with no verification that the token is actually a bead ID for the requested title. If the CLI prints any trailing word or echoes the title, this path can resolve the wrong ID and then attach dependencies/comments to the wrong bead or to a nonexistent bead.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1702-1730
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3237-3255
remediation_hint: Reuse the existing verified resolution strategy already used elsewhere in this file: treat stdout candidates as hints, then confirm them with `br show`/title matching before returning an ID, and only fall back to title lookup after that verification fails.

Comment writes in `materialize_beads` are not idempotent. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1552-1563`, the workstream description is unconditionally posted via `comment_bead(&workstream_epic.id, description)` on every call — even when the workstream epic was just matched (`created = false`). Likewise the per-bead planning rationale at lines 1591-1604 is posted every time `render_bead_planning_comment(...)` returns `Some`, with no guard against the bead being reused. The `materialize_beads_is_idempotent_for_existing_titles` test at lines 15156-15245 actually *codifies* this behavior: on a full rerun it accepts 8 fresh "comment added" mutations even though `created_beads == 0`. This directly violates the acceptance criterion "Idempotent: running twice doesn't create duplicates" and will accumulate duplicate rationale comments on every subsequent export (e.g. when one new bead is added to the plan and the user reruns export-beads, every previously-exported bead gets a second copy of its rationale). Gate the two `comment_bead` calls on `created == true` (or diff against the existing comment set via `br comments list --json`) so reused beads skip comment reposting. Update the idempotent test to assert zero comment mutations on rerun.

Cross-milestone title collisions silently reuse unrelated beads. `materialize_beads` builds `existing_by_title` from `BrCommand::list_all()` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1491-1504`), which returns every bead in the repo. `materialize_bead_entry` (lines 1659-1694) then pops a match by normalized title alone — no filter by milestone, label, or type. Consequences: (1) the root epic is materialized with `bundle.identity.name` (e.g. "Alpha") as its title and no scoping, so a pre-existing bead titled "Alpha" from an unrelated milestone is reused as this milestone's root; (2) workstream epics use generic names like "Core" or "Validation" that are highly likely to collide across milestones — the exporter will link this milestone's new tasks under another milestone's "Core" epic; (3) the created root is labeled `milestone-root` but the match ignores labels, so the label does not protect against misreuse. Recommended: label every materialized bead with a milestone-scoped label (e.g. `milestone:<id>`) and filter `existing_by_title` to entries carrying that label before matching. Add a test where two milestones share a workstream name and the second export does not rebind to the first's epic.

Implicit bead IDs in `depends_on` cause export to fail. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1606-1608`, `proposal_id_map` is populated only when `proposal.bead_id.is_some()`. The final dependency loop at lines 1617-1644 looks up `proposal_id_map.get(&dependency)` and errors with "bead depends on unknown proposal id" on miss. However, `MilestoneBundle::validate` in Compatibility mode (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:91-410`) accepts proposals without an explicit `bead_id` — they get an implicit identity like `<milestone>.bead-N` and may be referenced from other proposals' `depends_on` using that implicit form. Running `materialize_beads` on such a valid bundle will fail mid-way (after creating root + workstream epics + some tasks) with no sync flush. Fix: when building `proposal_id_map`, also register the implicit canonical bead id (same form used by `backfill_legacy_explicit_bead_flags` / `normalize_bead_reference`), and have the dependency loop normalize each `depends_on` reference through the same function before lookup. Add a test bundle that uses implicit IDs in `depends_on`.

`handle_export_beads` records nothing in the milestone journal. `src/cli/milestone.rs:360-380` creates beads, comments, and dependency edges against `br`, then sets the active milestone and prints a summary — but it never appends a `MilestoneJournalEvent` recording that an export happened, what root/workstream/task IDs were produced, or which bundle hash was exported. Other mutating milestone commands (plan/run/next) keep a journal trail. Without an event here, forensics after a failed or partial export (e.g., to reconcile which IDs `br` owns for this milestone) has to reverse-engineer state from the `br` tracker alone. Consider appending a `BeadsExported` (or similar) journal event with the bundle hash and the `BeadMaterializationReport` details before returning.

finding_summary: The export path never creates the required parent-child hierarchy. materialize_beads() sends the milestone-root -> workstream and workstream -> task links through ensure_dependency() (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1579-1585, 1628-1635), and ensure_dependency() always calls BrMutationAdapter::add_dependency() (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1987-2003). That helper builds BrCommand::dep_add(from, to) without a dependency type (src/adapters/br_process.rs:415-417, 1372-1380), so br uses its default blocks edge instead of parent-child. The exported epic tree will not be recognized by downstream code that keys on DependencyKind::ParentChild, and the implementation misses the hierarchy required by the bead-export contract. affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, src/adapters/br_process.rs. remediation_hint: Thread dependency kind through the mutation API so root/workstream/task hierarchy edges use parent-child while BeadProposal.depends_on edges remain blocks.

finding_summary: A partial export failure leaves the new CLI retry path unusable. handle_export_beads() constructs a fresh BrMutationAdapter with a random adapter_id on every invocation (src/cli/milestone.rs:371-374; src/adapters/br_process.rs:817-819). After any successful mutation, exec_tracked_mutation_locked() persists a pending-mutation record and keeps it on subsequent failures (src/adapters/br_process.rs:1253-1286). The next export-beads run then hits ensure_bead_export_preflight(), which calls ensure_synced() and unconditionally refuses to proceed while that pending record exists (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1823-1834; src/adapters/br_process.rs:1107-1120). In practice, a transient failure after creating some beads forces a manual br sync --flush-only before the user can rerun the command, so the promised idempotent export flow is not actually recoverable from mid-run errors. affected_files: src/cli/milestone.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, src/adapters/br_process.rs. remediation_hint: Give export-beads a stable adapter id per milestone/export operation and/or add an explicit safe recovery path for its own pending mutations before preflight.

finding_summary: Reruns do not restore planning comments that were skipped by a mid-run failure. Workstream descriptions are only commented when workstream_epic.created is true (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1588-1600), and task planning rationale is only commented when bead.created is true (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1637-1654). If an export fails after creating or syncing a bead but before adding its comment, the next run reuses that bead and permanently skips the missing comment, yet still reports success. That leaves the exported milestone missing the rationale/acceptance-context the command is supposed to attach. affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs. remediation_hint: On reused beads, detect whether the expected planning/workstream comment is already present and add it when absent, or persist enough export state to replay comment steps safely.

finding_summary: Idempotent reruns break when a workstream name matches the milestone name. The root epic is created with labels [milestone:<id>, milestone-root] while workstream epics are created with only [milestone:<id>] (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1545-1551, 1566-1570). The reuse matcher treats an existing bead as compatible when it is a title/type match and contains all requested labels, not an exact label set (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1870-1888). Because bundle validation does not reject a workstream name equal to identity.name (src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:141-158), a valid bundle can contain both a root epic and a workstream epic with the same title. On the second export, find_existing_materialized_bead() removes both candidates under that shared title and then fails with an ambiguity error (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1894-1951), so the command is not actually idempotent for that valid input. affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs. remediation_hint: Match root epics and workstream epics with disjoint identity criteria (for example, require milestone-root on root reuse and exclude it from workstream reuse) or reject same-title milestone/workstream bundles during validation.

finding_summary: The new bead-export path deserializes `br show --json` into `BeadDetail`, but the real `br` CLI returns a one-element array. `materialize_beads()` does this on every create/reuse path (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1784-1802`, `2014-2023`), while `BrCommand::show()` always emits `br show <id> --json` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:352-355`) and `exec_json()` blindly parses into the requested type (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:769-779`). I verified locally in this repo that `br show ralph-burning-9ni.4.6 --json | jq -r 'type'` returns `array`. As written, `milestone export-beads` will raise `BrParseError` the first time it tries to resolve a created bead or inspect an existing one. The new tests miss this because the helpers at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5235-5300` mock `show` as an object rather than the real array payload.

finding_summary: The idempotency/reuse logic only treats `open` and `in_progress` beads as reusable, and converts any closed/deferred milestone-scoped title match into a hard failure. `summary_matches_materialized_bead_except_role()` filters matches to `Open | InProgress` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1904-1908`), then `find_existing_materialized_bead()` returns an error when no eligible match remains (`1968-1981`) or when a valid match coexists with a non-active historical duplicate (`1984-2007`). In practice, rerunning `milestone export-beads` after some previously exported beads have been completed or deferred will fail instead of skipping those existing beads and exporting newly added work, which breaks the command's expected idempotent/incremental behavior.

finding_summary: `milestone export-beads` replays any pending mutation journal for the same milestone as if it belonged to the current invocation, so a stale or concurrent export can be flushed into `.beads` before the new bundle is processed.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/cli/milestone.rs:369-373
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1836-1845
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:113-119
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1181-1217
remediation_hint: Include the current bundle/plan hash and a per-attempt ownership token in the pending-mutation identity, and only replay a recovered export when that metadata matches the current invocation.

finding_summary: Export idempotency is keyed only by normalized title/role, so valid bundles that use explicit bead IDs but reuse a title cannot be re-exported; the second run hits the multiple-active-matches error even though the proposals are distinct.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:221-233
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1530-1541
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1551-1553
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1666-1670
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1942-2009
remediation_hint: Use canonical proposal IDs as the durable idempotency key, for example by persisting and consulting a proposal `bead_id` -> actual bead-id mapping instead of matching only on title.

finding_summary: `materialize_beads()` assumes `br list --all --json` is a bare `Vec<BeadSummary>` at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1526-1530`, and its fallback matcher makes the same assumption at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3671-3680`. On the installed `br` CLI, `br list --all --json` returns an object wrapper (`{"issues": [...]}`), so the export path fails with a parse error before it can check for existing beads or create anything.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Add a wrapper deserializer for `br list` responses (or normalize the envelope in the adapter) and update the tests to use the real `{"issues": [...]}` payload shape.

finding_summary: Every export create call includes milestone labels because `materialized_bead_labels()` always prepends `milestone:<id>` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1903-1918`), but `create_bead()` still serializes labels as repeated `--label=...` flags (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:1341-1346`). The installed `br create` command rejects that flag in favor of `--labels`, so the first real create exits immediately and no beads can be materialized.
affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_process.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Serialize labels using the CLI's supported `--labels` contract and add an integration-shaped test that validates the actual command line emitted for labeled creates.

finding_summary: The new export path drops the only proposal_id -> actual br bead_id mapping, so downstream milestone execution still cannot recognize exported beads. `materialize_beads()` builds `proposal_id_map` only in memory and never persists it; the returned/reportable state is reduced to flat ID lists (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1569-1571`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1697-1702`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1765-1770`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3932-3989`). But the controller and CLI continue to decide milestone membership by comparing `br ready` / `br show` IDs against the plan refs from `plan.json` (`src/cli/run.rs:294-333`, `src/cli/run.rs:452-464`, `src/cli/run.rs:721-744`, `src/cli/milestone.rs:1530-1585`). Because `br create` generates repository issue IDs instead of the plan proposal refs, a successful export leaves `milestone next`/`milestone run` with zero matching ready beads. remediation_hint: Persist a durable proposal-ref -> live-bead-id mapping that downstream lookups read, or create beads under stable plan IDs so runtime membership checks can resolve exported beads back to the plan.

finding_summary: Idempotent export is broken for valid bundles with duplicate workstream names, because workstream epics have no stable per-workstream identity. They are created with only the milestone label and no disambiguator (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1598-1614`), while the reuse path treats multiple active same-title matches as a hard error (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2023-2055`). The bundle model already allows duplicate workstream names and renders them with ordinal display labels (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1283-1309`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:3273-3300`), so a plan can export successfully once and then fail on the required second run. remediation_hint: Give each workstream epic a deterministic identity for reuse, analogous to `proposal:` labels for task beads, so repeated exports can distinguish same-title workstreams.

In src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs around lines 1597-1614 (the workstream-epic creation loop inside materialize_beads), workstream epics are matched to existing beads only by (milestone_scope_label, type=epic, title) via existing_by_title. Task beads use canonical_export_proposal_id to add a per-proposal `proposal:<id>` label that distinguishes them, but workstream epics have no analogous disambiguator. bundle.validate() intentionally permits duplicate workstream names (see contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs tests plan_md_disambiguates_duplicate_workstream_names and the pub(crate) export of normalize_bead_reference / canonicalize_bead_reference). As a result, a plan with two workstreams sharing a title will either (a) create two distinct beads on the first run that both match the same (title, scope) key on re-run, causing find_existing_materialized_bead to fail with `active_eligible_indices.len() > 1`, or (b) collapse the second workstream into the first on first run, silently merging their tasks under the wrong parent. Please give workstream epics the same treatment as task beads: canonicalize the workstream reference (title + positional index within the bundle) into a stable workstream-scoped label such as `workstream:<canonical-id>`, attach it on creation, and match on it in find_existing_materialized_bead. The proposal_id_map keying can then stay title-based for bead dependencies that reference the workstream by name, but re-run identity should not depend on titles alone.

finding_summary: Explicit-bead exports never reuse the already-authored bead ID, so bundles with `explicit_id=true` can create a duplicate bead instead of wiring the existing issue into the milestone.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
evidence:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:615-620 documents that explicit beads may already exist in `.beads/` under their short IDs.
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1543-1561 only indexes existing candidates that already carry the `milestone:<id>` label.
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1648-1676 uses `proposal.bead_id` only to derive proposal labels/report keys; it never probes that authored ID for reuse.
impact:
A milestone plan that intentionally points at an existing bead will be exported onto a fresh duplicate instead. Dependencies and planning comments then land on the duplicate while the real authored bead stays outside the milestone graph.
remediation_hint: When `explicit_id` is true, resolve that authored bead first (for example via `br show <bead_id>`) and materialize against its live ID before falling back to title-based matching.

finding_summary: Re-export is over-constrained by mutable proposal labels, so adding a label to an already-exported bead turns the existing identity-labeled bead into an "incompatible active match" and aborts the export.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs
evidence:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2016-2028 requires every non-identity label from the current proposal to already exist on the summary/detail before a bead is eligible for reuse.
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2127-2143 converts the remaining active bead into a hard error instead of reusing the bead that still carries the same `proposal:<id>` / `workstream:<id>` identity.
impact:
A normal plan edit such as adding or removing a task label after the first export breaks replay/idempotency for that proposal even though the identity label still uniquely identifies the same logical bead.
remediation_hint: Treat the proposal/workstream identity label as the primary reuse key and tolerate metadata drift there, or reconcile label changes explicitly instead of rejecting the export.

finding_summary: Short explicit `bead_id` values can bind to the wrong existing bead. `find_explicit_materialized_bead()` probes the raw short id before the milestone-qualified form and accepts the first successful `br show` result without checking that it belongs to the current milestone or matches the canonicalized ref (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2256-2261, 2276-2282`). Because `BeadProposal.bead_id` is documented as a stable id or suffix, a plan entry like `bead-2` should resolve within the current milestone; today it can instead reuse an unrelated bead that happens to share that short id, and the export will then attach parent-child links and planning comments to the wrong issue. affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"] remediation_hint: Prefer the milestone-qualified candidate first, or validate that any raw-id hit resolves back to the current milestone/proposal before reusing it.

finding_summary: Explicit-id matches are not removed from the same-title reuse cache, so two distinct proposals can collapse onto one existing bead during a single export. `materialize_bead_entry()` returns immediately when `find_explicit_materialized_bead()` succeeds (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1796-1800, 2247-2273`), which leaves that bead's summary in `existing_by_title`. Later, `find_existing_materialized_bead()` can reuse the same summary for another proposal with the same title because `identity_label_matches()` treats beads that have no materialized identity label as matching any proposal/workstream label (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1936-1950, 2127-2219`). A legacy or manually labeled explicit bead that only carries `milestone:*` can therefore satisfy two plan nodes, collapsing `planned_bead_id_map` and dependency wiring onto one actual bead. affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"] remediation_hint: When an explicit-id lookup succeeds, either remove that bead from `existing_by_title` immediately or tighten same-title fallback so unlabeled legacy beads cannot be reused for a second distinct proposal in the same run.

finding_summary: `find_explicit_materialized_bead()` treats every `BrExitError` from `br show` as if the explicit bead were simply missing. In [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2269) through [service.rs:2280](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2280), a locked/corrupt `.beads` state or any other non-not-found `br show` failure is silently ignored, and export can then fall through to title matching or fresh creation. That can duplicate an explicitly anchored bead or bind the proposal to the wrong existing bead instead of surfacing the repository error.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2269-2280"]
remediation_hint: Only continue when the `BrExitError` is a real not-found response (for example by reusing `is_missing_bead_error`); propagate all other `br show` failures.

finding_summary: Export trusts planner-supplied labels even when they collide with reserved export metadata. `materialized_bead_labels()` in [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1952) blindly preserves user labels, while later matching logic in [service.rs:1932-1950](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1932) and [service.rs:2065-2076](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2065) treats `proposal:*`, `workstream:*`, and `milestone-root` as trusted system labels. A bundle that emits one of those labels on a normal task/workstream can make reruns misclassify the bead as the root or as another proposal, leading to incorrect reuse or spurious ambiguity failures.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1932-1967", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2065-2076"]
remediation_hint: Reject or strip reserved export-label namespaces during bundle validation/export so planner-authored labels cannot spoof exporter identity metadata.

In `src/adapters/br_process.rs:1341-1344`, `BrMutationAdapter::create_bead` joins labels with `,` and passes them as a single `--labels=a,b,c` argument. Bundle validation (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:205-207`) and the materialization label builder only reject empty labels — a label value containing a literal `,` would be silently split into two labels by the `br` CLI. Since bead label strings flow from the (often LLM-authored) plan bundle through `materialized_bead_labels()` into this adapter unchanged, a stray comma in a plan label would silently corrupt the labels applied to the created bead without any error path catching it. Either reject labels containing `,` in bundle validation, reject them in `create_bead`, or revert to the previous repeated `--label=...` form so label values are passed opaquely.

finding_summary: `materialize_beads()` cannot reliably distinguish a workstream epic from a task bead when both are non-root epics. `identity_label_matches()` treats any legacy bead that is missing exporter identity labels as a match, and `materialized_role_matches()` collapses `WorkstreamEpic` and `Task` into the same bucket. On a rerun against an older or label-drifted export, a proposal with `bead_type: "epic"` and the same title as its workstream can therefore reuse the workstream epic as the task bead; the later parent-child link then targets the same bead and corrupts the hierarchy. This comes from the reuse path in `find_existing_materialized_bead()` relying on those predicates. affected_files: `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1936-1950`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2034-2070`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2117-2159`. remediation_hint: make role matching distinguish workstream epics from task beads even for legacy unlabeled beads, or fail closed instead of allowing unlabeled non-root epics to satisfy task reuse.

finding_summary: explicit bead-id reuse accepts any canonical/short-id hit before title matching, without validating that the existing bead still matches the proposal’s type or role. In `find_explicit_materialized_bead()`, any `br show` result for the explicit id is accepted if `explicit_materialized_bead_matches()` returns true; that helper only checks the id and milestone/identity labels, not whether the bead still looks like the proposal being exported. A stale or mistyped `bead_id` in the bundle can therefore hijack an unrelated existing bead and attach the current workstream dependency/comments to it. affected_files: `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2259-2321`. remediation_hint: after resolving an explicit bead by id, still require at least bead-type/role compatibility (and ideally some title or provenance sanity check), while preserving the ability to tolerate title drift for genuinely reused authored beads.

finding_summary: Export ignores an authored `explicit_id=true` when the supplied bead id happens to equal the proposal's implicit slot. `materialize_beads()` always derives the explicit lookup candidate through `explicit_export_bead_id()` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1650`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2011-2030`), and that helper drops any id that normalizes to `bead-{implicit_index}`. But the bundle model explicitly allows `explicit_id=true` even for slot-matching ids (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:541-567`). In that supported shape, export will skip the id-based reuse path, fall back to title matching, and can duplicate a bead or attach dependencies/comments to the wrong issue after a rename.
affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
remediation_hint: Make `explicit_export_bead_id()` honor `proposal.explicit_id == Some(true)` before suppressing slot-matching ids, so explicitly authored `bead-1`/`ms-foo.bead-1` values still drive reuse by id.

finding_summary: Runtime bead membership can resurrect a stale export map from history whenever the current plan hash matches an older export, even if a newer `beads_exported` event for a different plan exists. `load_runtime_bead_membership_refs()` extends membership from `latest_exported_bead_id_map()` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4255-4258`), but that helper scans backward until it finds any matching `bundle_hash` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4262-4280`). If the milestone was exported for plan A, later exported for plan B, and then `plan.json` returns to the old A hash without a fresh export, the controller will reuse A's historical actual bead ids as if they were current. That can make `run` treat stale beads as in-scope until the user re-exports.
affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs
remediation_hint: Only reuse the exported id map when the newest `beads_exported` event overall matches the current plan hash; if a later export has a different hash, treat the current plan as not yet exported and return an empty map.
